description: The Sisters of Service were founded in 1922 to assist the influx of immigrants into Canada since the beginning of the 20th century. The idea of the Roman Catholic community of Sisters came from Catherine Donnelly, an Ontario teacher. Although the headquarters were in Toronto, the Sisters served across Canada in health care, education, social work and immigration. For 60 years, their work in 56 missions was captured on the pages of The Field at Home, their quarterly magazine. Materials for this collection provided by: Sisters of Service
title: Archives of the Sisters of Service
